description: Dead tuples accumulate because autovacuum can't keep up or a long transaction pins the xmin horizon, bloating tables and indexes.
body: Failure mode: a high-churn table's UPDATE/DELETE traffic creates dead tuples faster than autovacuum reclaims them, or an idle-in-transaction session or replication slot holds back the xmin horizon so VACUUM cannot remove recent dead tuples; scans slow and storage grows. Detection signals: rising n_dead_tup in pg_stat_user_tables; old last_autovacuum; a long-lived transaction in pg_stat_activity. Do not run VACUUM FULL on a hot table without a window (it locks exclusively). Safer fallback: tune per-table autovacuum to trigger sooner, kill stuck long transactions, and monitor dead tuples back down.
